Hero.name: Tobias Ludwig
Hero.valueProposition: I combine 10+ years of IT practice with a clear BA mindset: from field operations and IT service to security-critical systems and software projects. My focus today is to analyze complex processes and translate them into implementable requirements.

llm.label: LLM-optimized
llm.desc: Plain text for AI parsing and analysis

Hero.focusLine: Experience in analyzing and optimizing processes in security-critical IT environments

Metrics.experienceContext: From fiber optics to SINA to software development
Metrics.projectsContext: Independently designed and run in production
Metrics.certificatesContext: Incl. ITIL, SINA CORE, SINA BASICS

WhyBitmarck.heading: Why Bitmarck
WhyBitmarck.card1Description: What motivates me about Bitmarck is the concrete impact: healthcare digitization that directly affects millions of insured people. That is exactly the environment where I want to take ownership.
WhyBitmarck.card2Description: I bring a fitting foundation: SINA experience from two security-critical environments and a self-designed auth service with RBAC — closely aligned with bitIAM and telematics needs.
WhyBitmarck.card3Description: I work at the intersection: understanding business-side problems, structuring implementation-ready requirements, and aligning stakeholders across teams. That is exactly what this role at Bitmarck requires.

Career.heading: My Path to Business Analyst
Career.intro: My path moved from field technician work to security-critical infrastructure and then software delivery. The common thread stayed the same: analyze problems clearly, set priorities, and structure solutions so business and engineering can move together.

telekom.body: Fiber splicing and copper measurement (2-wire). Analyzed faults with OTDR, Murray method, and ORCA, then planned actions and coordinated civil engineering for incident resolution.
telekom.narrative: This is where my foundation was built: systematic root-cause analysis, prioritization, and translating findings into clear actions.
telekom.technologies: FTTH, Fiber Optics, Splicing, Measurement Technology
details: Systematic fault analysis with structured methodology
Quality assurance through measurement-based testing
Coordination with internal teams and external partners
Technical documentation of processes and results

detailPage.overview: My entry into the IT world at Germany's largest telecommunications company. In the fiber optic deployment program, I was responsible for installation, setup, and quality assurance of FTTH connections. What looks like manual work at first glance was actually a crash course in structured problem-solving.
detailPage.baRelevance: The ability to systematically narrow down technical problems and document solutions is the foundation of any requirements analysis. At Telekom, I learned that every problem has a root cause — and that you find it through structured methodology, not guessing.
achievements: Independent fault diagnosis and resolution with defined escalation process
Quality assurance through standardized measurement procedures (OTDR, power measurement)
Documentation of all installations for traceability
Collaboration with planning department and external contractors

mediacom.body: On-site service across NRW/Hesse with high ticket volume (~12/day). Evaluated signal metrics (level, MER, BER, ingress), narrowed causes, and handled complex B2B cases as expert technician.
mediacom.narrative: Data-based assessment and fast decision-making under pressure — a core pattern for analytical BA work.
mediacom.technologies: Coaxial Networks, MER, BER, Signal Analysis
details: Data-driven fault diagnosis based on signal metrics
Analysis and optimization of network quality (MER, BER)
Coordination with departments for root cause analysis
Systematic documentation of disruption patterns

detailPage.overview: At Media.com, I was responsible for maintaining and optimizing coaxial networks at network level 4. The focus was on analyzing signal quality using concrete metrics — MER (Modulation Error Ratio) and BER (Bit Error Ratio). This is where the technician first became an analyst.
detailPage.baRelevance: Working with signal metrics was my first systematic contact with data-driven decision making. I learned to recognize patterns from raw data, derive causes, and propose solutions — exactly what a business analyst does daily, just in a different context.
achievements: Developed a systematic approach to signal disruption analysis
Collaboration with network planners for sustainable quality improvement
Documentation of recurring disruption patterns for preventive measures
Translation of technical measurements for non-technical stakeholders

biermann.body: Progressed from technician to construction lead: copper network measurement, team coordination, civil works coordination, plus material planning and documentation under Telekom standards.
biermann.narrative: I learned to align technical execution, resources, and external partners toward one shared outcome.
biermann.technologies: Copper Network, Civil Engineering, Project Planning
details: Independent project planning and resource coordination
Leading technician teams (5–10 people)
Alignment with clients and external service providers
Technical documentation and progress tracking

detailPage.overview: As construction lead, I was responsible for planning, execution, and quality assurance of civil engineering works in telecommunications. I coordinated teams of 5–10 technicians, aligned with clients, and managed schedules and resources — essentially pure project management.
detailPage.baRelevance: Here I developed the ability to mediate between different stakeholders: clients with their requirements, technicians with their capacity, external companies with their schedules. Exactly this coordination skill is what a business analyst needs to translate between business and IT.
achievements: Independent management of parallel construction projects with team coordination
Balancing competing requirements between client and execution
Development of efficient workflows for schedule optimization
Quality assurance through structured acceptance protocols

bwi.body: Delivered SINA rollouts, handled incidents (INC/IMAC) with IBM Maximo, and coordinated with secunet in security-critical environments. Strong focus on structured execution, traceability, and secure client systems.
bwi.narrative: Understanding security requirements, documenting them clearly, and executing with multiple stakeholders — directly relevant to IAM at Bitmarck.
bwi.technologies: Cisco, LANCOM, VLAN, SINA, IBM Maximo
details: Working with security-critical SINA systems (directly relevant to IAM)
Structured support with IBM Maximo following ITIL processes
IT infrastructure projects with Ministry of Defence security requirements
Documentation and process optimization in regulated environments

detailPage.overview: At BWI, the IT service provider for the German armed forces, I worked on IT infrastructure projects for the Ministry of Defence. The focus was on security-critical systems, especially SINA (Secure Inter-Network Architecture) — the same technology used in the healthcare telematics infrastructure.
detailPage.baRelevance: BWI was my entry into regulated IT environments with high security requirements. Structured ITIL-based work, documented processes, and coordination between technical teams and departments — all directly transferable to working as a Business Analyst IAM in healthcare. My SINA experience is a direct bridge to Bitmarck.
achievements: Building deep SINA expertise in security-critical environment
Process-oriented ITIL-based work with structured ticketing
Coordination between technical teams and Ministry departments
Documentation of security requirements and configuration standards

bhf.subtitle: Fixed-term position · Parallel: Software Developer Expert training (IU)
bhf.body: Designed and delivered fitness administration software: gathered requirements, translated them into functionality, and structured delivery as a CRM/training solution while completing Software Developer Expert training.
bhf.narrative: Turning point toward BA work: combining problem understanding, solution definition, and delivery end-to-end in one role.
bhf.technologies: Requirements Analysis, Process Optimization, Node.js, PostgreSQL
details: Analysis of existing business processes and identification of optimization potential
Requirements gathering with stakeholders (management, trainers, clients)
Design and implementation of custom CRM solution
GDPR-compliant data processing and security concepts
Parallel: Software Developer Expert training (IU) completed

detailPage.overview: BHF Group was a fixed-term position — but one with maximum learning impact. In parallel, I completed my Software Developer Expert training at IU. In practice, I took full responsibility for a digitalization project: from analyzing existing business processes through requirements gathering to designing and implementing CRM and training software.
detailPage.baRelevance: This role was the turning point. For the first time, I wasn't on the tech side but at the intersection: management, trainers, and clients had different requirements that I had to prioritize, align, and translate into a technical solution. The parallel training provided the theoretical foundation. This is the core of the business analyst role.
achievements: Complete requirements analysis with three different stakeholder groups
Process modeling of existing workflows and identification of automation potential
Design and implementation of GDPR-compliant data processing
Parallel: Software Developer Expert training (IU) successfully completed

persona.subtitle: Temporary agency · Transitional role
persona.body: IT project coordination and process optimization focused on external vendor coordination, infrastructure topics, and clear documentation.
persona.narrative: Short engagement, clear takeaway: my strength is analysis, structuring, and coordination at the intersection of technology and organization.
details: Quick onboarding and analysis of existing processes
Coordination of IT projects and external service providers
Identification and implementation of process improvements
Structured documentation and knowledge transfer

detailPage.overview: A short engagement through a temporary agency, intended as a transitional solution. The work itself — IT coordination and process optimization — went well, but the role wasn't the right long-term perspective. This experience sharpened my clarity about the next career step.
detailPage.baRelevance: Even in this short engagement, my analytical approach proved its worth: quick onboarding, pragmatic prioritization, structured documentation. The experience confirmed that I'm most productive in roles that combine analytical thinking with IT understanding.
achievements: Quick analysis and documentation of existing IT processes
Coordination of infrastructure projects with multiple service providers
Implementation of process improvements within short timeframes
Gained clarity about professional direction

freelancer.role: Freelancer — DevOps, Web Development & IT Consulting
freelancer.subtitle: Part-time
freelancer.body: Part-time, I run client projects end-to-end: captured requirements, simplified processes, accelerated workflows through automation, and delivered web/server solutions with a strong focus on cost-efficiency and performance.
freelancer.narrative: Freelancing makes the BA perspective tangible: full ownership, precise requirement capture, clear decisions, and improvements with measurable impact.
freelancer.technologies: DevOps, Docker, Linux, Next.js, Web Development, Servers
details: Client meetings and project planning
Web development and hosting (concept to go-live)
Server setup and DevOps tasks
Independent project management and client communication

detailPage.overview: Since January 2026, I've been working part-time as a freelancer. I handle DevOps tasks, develop and operate websites, and set up servers for clients. In a one-person setup, I continuously optimize workflows, use automation where useful, and balance cost, performance, and maintainability.
detailPage.baRelevance: In freelancing, BA work is direct and accountable: I clarify needs with clients, break them into prioritized requirements, define the solution, and carry it into operations. Because I own the full scope, decisions must be analytically sound, economically sensible, and technically sustainable.
achievements: Independent client acquisition, requirements gathering, and project planning
End-to-end responsibility: from initial meeting to productive operation
Accelerated processes through automation and reduced operational overhead
DevOps and infrastructure: servers, Docker, CI/CD for client projects
Web development with modern stack (Next.js, Tailwind, self-hosting)

xecuro.role: IT System Administrator Secure Client Manager
xecuro.body: Administration of SINA-based secure clients and SINA CA, including analysis of security requirements and translation into traceable ticketing/documentation workflows (Jira/Confluence). Also includes customer alignment on the ongoing project within confidentiality limits.
xecuro.narrative: Here I combine security practice with BA execution: structuring requirements, aligning with teams and customers, and translating them into reliable processes and documentation.
xecuro.technologies: SINA, Windows, Linux, Hardening, Security Concepts
details: Administration and advancement of SINA-based secure clients
Active contribution to security concepts and access policies
Ticket and knowledge workflows with Jira and Confluence in daily operations
Customer alignment in project context under confidentiality constraints
Maintenance and updates of the internal intranet during idle periods
Analysis of security requirements and their technical implementation

detailPage.overview: Xecuro is a security-sector startup with high ownership and short decision paths. I administer SINA-based secure clients, work with SINA CA, and handle additional security-critical systems. In daily operations I work with Jira and Confluence and maintain internal intranet content during idle periods. I also align with customers on the ongoing project, while details remain limited due to confidentiality.
detailPage.baRelevance: This role is a direct bridge to Bitmarck IAM: SINA systems, security concepts, SINA CA, and access policies are core IAM topics. It is also BA work in practice: analyzing security requirements, translating them into structured tickets and documentation, and aligning with involved teams. This is exactly the mix of analysis, structure, and security expected for the role.
achievements: Deepened SINA expertise: secure clients, SINA CA, and security-critical systems
Analyzed security requirements and translated them into clear process steps
Concrete process work: structured ticketing and documentation workflows in Jira/Confluence
Regular customer alignment in project context (confidential setting)
Contributed actively to security concepts in a startup environment
Independently drove internal process optimization

bitmarck.body: The logical consequence: Analytical thinking, technical understanding, and SINA experience as an official role — shaping digital identities in healthcare.
bitmarck.narrative: All threads come together here — technology, analysis, security, coordination.
details: Technical specifications for IAM systems (bitIAM)
Requirements alignment with health insurers and telematics stakeholders
Process modeling and use case development
Translation between business and IT development

detailPage.overview: The Business Analyst IAM role at Bitmarck is the logical consequence of my entire career. All threads come together here: technical understanding from 10+ years in IT, SINA experience from security-critical environments, analytical thinking from every project, and the ability to translate between technology and business.
detailPage.baRelevance: Shaping digital insured identities in healthcare, aligning requirements from health insurers and telematics, creating technical specifications for bitIAM — this is exactly the intersection my entire career has prepared me for.
achievements: 10+ years IT experience with increasing analytical focus
Practical SINA expertise from two security-critical environments
Proven ability for independent requirements analysis and process modeling
Technical understanding that bridges business and development

Projects.heading: Case Studies from Practice
Projects.intro: Every project follows the same logic: understand the problem, analyze requirements, define the solution, deliver the result.

Skills.heading: Stack for This Role
Skills.intro: My competency profile combines analytical methods with technical depth — the ideal combination for a business analyst with an IT background.

categoryDescriptions.iam: Practical experience with identity and access management from own projects and security-critical environments.
categoryDescriptions.ba: Analytical methods and tools that I've applied in every project — from requirements gathering to process optimization.
categoryDescriptions.devops: Infrastructure know-how that enables me to realistically assess technical requirements and communicate at eye level with developers.
categoryDescriptions.languages: Programming languages and frameworks that I've used productively in own projects — not theoretical knowledge, but practice.

iam.title: IAM & Security — Competency Details
iam.overview: My IAM experience isn't textbook theory but grown practice: I built my own auth service with JWT and RBAC, administered SINA systems in security-critical environments, and actively contributed to security concepts.

SINA.description: Secure Inter-Network Architecture — practical experience from BWI (armed forces) and Xecuro. Administration, hardening, and concept work with the system also used in the healthcare telematics infrastructure.

JWT.description: JSON Web Tokens — independently implemented token flow with access and refresh token rotation in the Auth API Service. Understanding of token lifecycle, signing, and validation.

RBAC.description: Role-Based Access Control — fine-grained permission system with configurable roles and rights. From access model design to implementation.

RLS.description: Row-Level Security — multi-tenant data separation at database level with Supabase/PostgreSQL. Ensuring each user only sees their own data.

AuthSystems.description: Holistic understanding of authentication and authorization systems: session management, token-based auth, OAuth flows, multi-factor authentication.

ApiDesign.description: RESTful API design with focus on security, documentation, and error handling. Understanding of API gateways and interface specifications.
ApiDesign.context: Auth API Service, Next CMS, Logbuch App

ba.title: BA Methods — Competency Details
ba.overview: Analytical methods aren't just my toolkit — they're my way of thinking. In every project, I've gathered requirements, analyzed processes, coordinated stakeholders, and documented solutions.

Anforderungsanalyse.description: Systematic gathering and documentation of requirements with various stakeholder groups. Prioritization by business value, alignment of competing requirements, translation into technical specifications.

UseCases.description: Creating use cases and user stories as a bridge between business and development. Deriving test criteria from use cases for quality assurance.
UseCases.context: Auth API, MyFitCoach PRO, Next CMS

DSGVO.description: Practical experience with GDPR-compliant data processing: privacy-by-design, data minimization, consent management, deletion concepts. Directly relevant for health data.

Prozessoptimierung.description: Analysis of existing business processes, identification of inefficiencies and automation potential, design of optimized workflows.

Dokumentation.description: Structured technical and business documentation: API docs, process descriptions, decision protocols, handover documents.

StakeholderManagement.description: Coordination of diverse stakeholder groups with different interests. Moderation of requirement conflicts, expectation management, regular reporting.

devops.title: DevOps & Infra — Competency Details
devops.overview: My infrastructure know-how enables me to realistically assess technical requirements and communicate at eye level with developers. I understand what happens behind the scenes — and can therefore write better requirements.

Docker.description: Container-based deployments for all own projects. Docker Compose for multi-service architectures, self-hosting on own servers.
Docker.context: Next CMS, Auth API, this website

Linux.description: System administration, scripting, server hardening. Production systems operated and maintained on Linux.

Cisco.description: Network configuration, VLAN segmentation, routing and switching in enterprise environments.

LANCOM.description: LANCOM routers and switches configured and managed in security-critical networks.

Git.description: Version control with Git and GitHub: branching strategies, pull requests, code reviews, CI/CD integration.

languages.title: Languages & Frameworks — Competency Details
languages.overview: Programming isn't my main focus as a business analyst — but it gives me a decisive advantage: I understand what's technically feasible, can realistically estimate effort, and speak the developers' language.

TypeScript.description: Primary language for backend and frontend development. Type-safe codebases with strict configuration.
TypeScript.context: Auth API, Next CMS, this website

Flutter.description: Cross-platform mobile development with Dart. Three production apps built and published.

SQL.description: Database design, complex queries, performance optimization. PostgreSQL with Prisma and Supabase.

NextJs.description: React-based fullstack framework. Server-side rendering, API routes, App Router.
NextJs.context: Next CMS, MyFitCoach Dashboard, this website

location.question: Location
location.answer: Relocation to the Essen area or any Bitmarck location is already planned and not an issue. Flexibility for remote and on-site work is given.

startDate.question: Start Date
startDate.answer: 3 months notice period — available immediately after.

whyBitmarck.question: Why Bitmarck?
whyBitmarck.answer: Bitmarck digitizes healthcare — a domain with real impact on millions of insured people. The Business Analyst IAM role connects my SINA experience, my understanding of auth architectures, and my analytical approach with a task that truly matters: making digital insured identities secure and usable.

whyBA.question: Why Business Analyst?
whyBA.answer: Analytical thinking has been present in every one of my roles — from data-driven signal analysis to project coordination to independent requirements gathering. Business Analyst makes this strength an official role. And my technical background ensures I understand both sides.

experience.question: Do I have BA experience?
experience.answer: Yes — not by title, but in substance. At BHF Group, I analyzed business processes, gathered requirements with stakeholders, and translated them into software. In my own projects, I defined use cases, designed data models, and developed access concepts. And at BWI and Xecuro, I worked in regulated environments with documented processes.

tools.question: Confluence & Jira?
tools.answer: Yes — hands-on Jira and Confluence experience at Xecuro, plus IBM Maximo at BWI. I work confidently with ticket workflows, documentation, and clean handoffs between business and engineering.

Contact.heading: Contact
Contact.intro: If you are looking for someone who structures requirements, realistically assesses technical impact, and translates both into executable solutions, I would value the conversation.
Contact.directIntro: You can also contact me directly through the following channels.
Contact.name: Name
Contact.subject: Subject
Contact.successMessage: Thank you! Your message has been sent successfully. I will get back to you shortly.
Contact.errorMessage: An error occurred while sending. Please try again or contact me directly via email.

Footer.builtWith: Built with Next.js, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion, TypeScript, next-intl · Self-hosted with Docker
Footer.signature: Custom-built for Bitmarck · Business Analyst IAM · April 2026 · v1.0

Terminal.unknown: Command not found: {cmd}. Type 'help' for a list of commands.

authApi.title: Auth API Service
authApi.description: Web auth API implemented in TypeScript with JWT and session management.
authApi.problem: Goal: build an own auth backend component for login, token issuance, and session handling.
authApi.approach: Implemented with TypeScript/Node.js: JWT-based authentication, session management, and API endpoints for login and token handling.
authApi.result: Working auth API for web applications with login, token handling, and sessions.
authApi.highlights:
JWT handling for authentication
Session management in the backend
TypeScript implementation of auth endpoints
Basic login flow including token issuance
authApi.learnings: Practical deepening in auth flows, token handling, and session management.

nextCms.title: Next CMS
nextCms.description: Client requirements analyzed, architecture designed, and multi-tenant CMS implemented — from gathering to self-hosting.
nextCms.problem: Clients needed a flexible content management system without the complexity and cost of WordPress or Strapi. Core requirements: simple operation for non-technical users, fast performance, and independent hosting capability.
nextCms.approach: Requirements gathering with non-technical clients, translation into technical specification. Headless architecture with multi-tenant data separation (Row-Level Security). Setup automation via auto-install for non-technical users. Docker-based self-hosting with focus on cost control and performance.
nextCms.result: Fully functional CMS in production use by multiple clients with different requirements. Key insight: A solution's quality is not measured by technical complexity but by how well it meets user requirements.
nextCms.highlights:
Row-Level Security (RLS) for complete multi-tenant data separation at database level
Auto-install script for one-click setup without technical knowledge
Docker-based self-hosting with automated backups on own server
Production use by multiple clients with different requirements
nextCms.learnings: Requirements analysis for non-technical clients taught me to translate complex systems into simple usage concepts — a core competency for communication between business and technology.

myfitcoach.title: MyFitCoach PRO
myfitcoach.description: Stakeholder requirements from trainers and clients prioritized, multi-platform architecture designed and implemented across all layers.
myfitcoach.problem: Personal trainers and coaches needed a digital platform combining training planning, client management, progress tracking, and communication — with different requirements per user role (trainers vs. clients).
myfitcoach.approach: Stakeholder analysis with two user groups. Prioritization of competing requirements and derivation of a multi-platform strategy: mobile app for daily use, web dashboard for administration and analytics. Role-based views for different user groups.
myfitcoach.result: Working coaching platform with iOS/Android app and web dashboard. The key to success was prioritization: not everything for everyone, but the right thing for each role.
myfitcoach.highlights:
Multi-platform architecture: Flutter mobile app, Next.js web dashboard, and Strapi backend
Integrated analytics dashboard with progress charts and export functionality
Realtime features for live updates between trainer and client
Role-based views for trainers, clients, and administration
myfitcoach.learnings: Stakeholder management across different user groups (trainers vs. clients) demonstrated how important it is to prioritize competing requirements and find sustainable compromises.

partnerApp.title: Partner App
partnerApp.description: Privacy requirements analyzed, privacy-by-design architecture designed, and secure community platform with end-to-end encryption implemented.
partnerApp.problem: A private community needed a protected platform for communication and media exchange with the highest privacy requirements — end-to-end encryption, GDPR compliance, and no third-party platform dependency.
partnerApp.approach: Privacy requirements analysis following privacy-by-design principles. Architecture with end-to-end encryption, secure vault system for media, Riverpod state management, and Supabase backend. Content moderation and reporting system for community safety. GDPR-compliant data handling.
partnerApp.result: Production mobile app with active user base and end-to-end privacy concept. From requirements analysis through security architecture to moderation system, independently designed — directly transferable to GDPR compliance in healthcare.
partnerApp.highlights:
End-to-end encryption with secure vault system for sensitive media
Privacy-by-design architecture with GDPR-compliant data handling
Content moderation and reporting system for community safety
Riverpod state management for reactive, testable app architecture
partnerApp.learnings: Privacy-by-design and moderation workflows sharpened my understanding of data protection requirements — from concept to technical implementation, directly transferable to GDPR compliance in healthcare.

logbuchApp.title: Logbuch App
logbuchApp.description: Logbook app implemented with dynamic forms and data export.
logbuchApp.problem: A mobile solution for structured data capture was needed.
logbuchApp.approach: Implemented an app with dynamic forms, validations, and an interface for exporting captured data.
logbuchApp.result: Functional logbook app with structured capture and export functionality.
logbuchApp.highlights:
Dynamic form fields with validations
Structured data capture in the app
Data export via API
Focus on simple day-to-day usability
logbuchApp.learnings: This project improved my understanding of mobile data capture and clean data flows.

mpaNutrition.title: MPA Nutrition Shop
mpaNutrition.description: E-commerce business processes modeled end-to-end: order process, payment processing, shipping logic, and marketing.
mpaNutrition.problem: A nutrition company needed their own online shop without dependency on Shopify or WooCommerce. Core requirement: full control over design, payment flow, customer data, and marketing measures.
mpaNutrition.approach: End-to-end analysis of e-commerce business processes: product management, cart logic, payment processing (Stripe), shipping with tracking, customer account management. Additionally: marketing tools (discount codes, newsletter) and integrated analytics dashboard — with focus on efficient operations and stable performance.
mpaNutrition.result: Functional online shop with complete order flow in production. The end-to-end modeling of all business processes — from cart through payment to shipping — was the most valuable learning.
mpaNutrition.highlights:
Stripe payment integration with complete checkout flow and error handling
Shipping logic with tracking and automatic status updates
Marketing tools: discount codes, newsletter system, and analytics dashboard
Customer accounts with order history and session-based cart
mpaNutrition.learnings: Analyzing e-commerce business processes — from cart through payment to shipping — sharpened my understanding of end-to-end process modeling and business requirements.

dbfv.title: DBFV Competition Software
dbfv.description: Volunteer development of competition software for the German Bodybuilding & Fitness Federation (DBFV). Scoring system, athlete management, jury interface.
dbfv.problem: DBFV needed a digital solution for conducting bodybuilding competitions: athlete registration, real-time jury scoring, result calculation, and rankings.
dbfv.approach: Design and development of competition software: athlete and competition data management, digital scoring system for the jury, automatic result calculation and ranking generation.
dbfv.result: Functional competition software in volunteer use at DBFV. Complete competition workflow digitized: registration, scoring, results.
dbfv.highlights:
Digital scoring system for jury with real-time input
Automatic result calculation and ranking generation
Athlete management with competition history
Volunteer engagement for the German Bodybuilding & Fitness Federation
dbfv.learnings: Requirements gathering with jury members and competition organizers showed how important it is to understand domain processes before building a technical solution.

CertificationDetail.heading: Certifications & Qualifications in Detail
CertificationDetail.intro: Each certification represents targeted competency building — from IT service management to security architectures to infrastructure know-how. Here you can see what each qualification covers and why it is relevant for the Business Analyst IAM role.

sinaCore.name: SINA CORE
sinaCore.description: Advanced certification for Secure Inter-Network Architecture (SINA). Covers architecture concepts, cryptography fundamentals, network segmentation, and administration of SINA components in classified environments. Includes thin clients, SINA workstations, and central management systems.
sinaCore.baRelevance: SINA is the foundational technology for secure communication in the healthcare telematics infrastructure. As a Business Analyst IAM at Bitmarck, understanding this architecture is essential for correctly specifying requirements for secure identity systems.

sinaBasics.name: SINA BASICS
sinaBasics.description: Foundation certification for SINA systems. Covers core concepts of secure network communication, classified information handling, basic SINA components, and their interaction in security-critical infrastructures.
sinaBasics.baRelevance: The foundation for working with security-critical systems. This knowledge enables proper classification of security requirements in the IAM context and communication at eye level with developers and security teams.

itil.name: ITIL Foundation
itil.description: Certification in the ITIL IT Service Management framework. Covers the service lifecycle (Service Strategy, Design, Transition, Operation, Continual Improvement), incident, problem, and change management, as well as service level agreements and process documentation.
itil.baRelevance: ITIL processes are the foundation for structured IT service management at Bitmarck. As a business analyst, I use ITIL concepts for requirements gathering, process modeling, and aligning service level requirements with health insurers.

devops.name: DevOps Fundamentals
devops.description: Fundamentals of DevOps culture and practices: CI/CD pipelines, Infrastructure as Code, monitoring, automated testing, and collaboration between development and operations. Containerization with Docker and orchestration.
devops.baRelevance: Understanding DevOps processes enables realistic specification of deployment, monitoring, and operations requirements — a decisive advantage when technical and business requirements need to be brought together.

databases.name: Databases
databases.description: Relational databases and SQL: database design, normalization, complex queries, indexing, transactions, and performance optimization. Practical experience with PostgreSQL.
databases.baRelevance: Data modeling is a core tool for business analysts. Understanding database structures enables precise requirements for data management, permission concepts (RLS), and data integrity in the IAM context.

sapErp.name: SAP ERP Basics
sapErp.description: Introduction to SAP ERP systems: module overview (FI, CO, MM, SD, HR), SAP GUI navigation, business processes in SAP, master data management, and basic transactions. Understanding of integration between SAP modules.
sapErp.baRelevance: SAP knowledge is relevant in the GKV IT landscape, as many health insurers use SAP-based systems. As a business analyst, this knowledge helps correctly specify interface requirements between IAM systems and existing ERP processes.

qualitaetssicherung.name: Quality Assurance in Software Processes
qualitaetssicherung.description: Methods and tools for software quality assurance: test planning, test levels (unit, integration, system tests), test automation, code reviews, static analysis, metrics, and quality standards (ISO 25010).
qualitaetssicherung.baRelevance: Quality assurance is an integral part of BA work: from defining acceptance criteria to deriving test cases from use cases to evaluating test results — directly relevant for specifying IAM systems.

softwaretechnik.name: Fundamentals of Industrial Software Engineering
softwaretechnik.description: Software engineering methods for industrial applications: process models (Waterfall, V-Model, Scrum), requirements management, software architecture, design patterns, UML modeling, and project management in software development.
softwaretechnik.baRelevance: The theoretical foundation for the BA role: process models, UML diagrams, and requirements management are daily tools. This knowledge enables structured requirements documents and precise communication with development teams.

businessAnalyst.name: Business Analyst Learning Path
businessAnalyst.description: Comprehensive learning path for business analysis competencies: requirements elicitation techniques, stakeholder analysis, process modeling (BPMN), user stories, acceptance criteria, business cases, gap analysis, and agile BA methods.
businessAnalyst.baRelevance: Direct competency building for the target role. The learning path systematically deepens the methods a Business Analyst IAM uses daily — from requirements elicitation to process modeling.

linuxAdmin.name: Linux System Administration
linuxAdmin.description: Linux system administration: user management, filesystems, network configuration, service management (systemd), shell scripting, security hardening, package management, and monitoring. Practical server management in production environments.
linuxAdmin.baRelevance: Linux is the operating system for many IAM components and server infrastructures. Understanding user management and permission concepts at the OS level is directly transferable to IAM requirements.

lancom.name: LANCOM Certification
lancom.description: Certification for LANCOM network components: router configuration, VPN setup, VLAN segmentation, firewall rules, and central management via LANCOM Management Cloud. Focus on security-critical networks.
lancom.baRelevance: Network segmentation and VPN concepts are building blocks for secure IAM architectures. Understanding network infrastructure helps formulate requirements for network security and access control correctly.

ftth.name: FTTH Fiber Optics
ftth.description: Fiber-to-the-Home technology: fiber splicing, OTDR measurement, attenuation measurement, network planning, and installation of fiber optic connections. Quality assurance through standardized measurement procedures.
ftth.baRelevance: The telecommunications foundation training instilled systematic work, structured fault analysis, and quality assurance — baseline competencies that are indispensable in any analytical role.

fitTrainer.name: Fitness Trainer B License
fitTrainer.description: Professional qualification as fitness trainer: training science, anatomy, nutrition science, training plan creation, and client support. Understanding of systematic goal pursuit and individual needs analysis.
fitTrainer.baRelevance: Seemingly unrelated, but at its core it's the same: individual needs analysis, goal definition, plan development, and progress monitoring. The same thinking applies to stakeholder requirements and project goals.

nutritionist.name: Certified Nutritionist
nutritionist.description: Qualification as nutritional consultant: nutritional physiology, macro- and micronutrients, nutrition planning, consulting methodology, and communication techniques.
nutritionist.baRelevance: The consulting methodology — needs analysis, goal setting, individual plan development, and success monitoring — is directly transferable to requirements gathering and stakeholder consulting in the BA context.

bsc.description: Ongoing computer science degree with focus areas in software engineering, theoretical computer science, databases, and algorithms. Systematically building the theoretical foundation for analytical work as a business analyst.

sde.description: Completed training with focus areas in software engineering, quality assurance, industrial software engineering, and project management. Provided the methodical toolkit for structured requirements analysis and process modeling.

itse.description: Three-year dual apprenticeship at Deutsche Telekom. Foundation in IT infrastructure, network technology, system administration, and structured problem-solving — the technical basis of my entire career.

JobMatch.heading: Requirements vs. Qualifications
JobMatch.intro: I intentionally mapped this role against my track record: from process analysis and structuring to delivery in security-critical IT environments.

digitalization.evidence: 6 independent software projects, CRM at BHF Group, and accelerated workflows in freelance projects through automation

requirements.evidence: Hands-on requirement clarification with stakeholders (e.g., BHF, freelance projects) plus SINA context from BWI/Xecuro

useCases.evidence: User stories and workflow descriptions in own projects; formal use-case documents used only selectively

bpmn.evidence: Processes analyzed and improved in practice; BPMN currently in learning path, so far rarely modeled formally

testing.evidence: Quality assurance in every project, ITIL Foundation

tools.evidence: Jira + Confluence at Xecuro, IBM Maximo at BWI, structured ticketing and documentation

analytical.evidence: Whether in measurement operations, SINA environments, or own projects, I work in a structured flow: root-cause analysis, prioritization, then implementable solutions.

Interactive.pageIntro: Get to know me in a playful way -- three mini-games that show why I'm a great fit for Bitmarck.

quiz.title: Requirements Check
quiz.resultDescription: Many requirements are well covered. Formal BA methods are currently being deepened further.

q1.question: Does Tobias have hands-on experience with SINA systems?
q1.match: Yes -- at BWI (armed forces) and Xecuro, in two security-critical environments.

q2.question: Can he gather and prioritize requirements from different stakeholders?
q2.match: Yes -- at BHF Group with management, trainers, and clients. In own projects with end users.

q3.question: Does he understand IAM architectures like JWT, RBAC, and token flows?
q3.match: Yes -- he built his own auth service with JWT rotation and fine-grained RBAC.

q4.question: Does he have experience with ITIL processes and structured work?
q4.match: Yes -- ITIL Foundation certified, practical IBM Maximo experience at BWI, and Jira/Confluence workflows at Xecuro.

q5.question: Is he willing to relocate to Essen?
q5.match: Yes -- relocation to the Essen area is already planned.

careerExplorer.title: Career Explorer
careerExplorer.startText: Click through 10+ years of IT career and discover interesting facts.

telekom.funFact: Promoted from construction crew to measurement team -- because analytical thinking mattered more than manual labor.

mediacom.funFact: 12 tickets per day in the field -- and still promoted to expert for the tough cases.

biermann.funFact: Led teams of 5-10 people, coordinated civil engineering -- classic project management without the title.

bwi.funFact: First contact with SINA systems -- the same technology used in the healthcare telematics infrastructure.

bhf.funFact: First time owning the complete BA cycle independently: requirements, architecture, implementation.

xecuro.funFact: Deepened SINA expertise, structured security requirements, and translated them into reliable Jira/Confluence workflows -- direct IAM bridge.

bitmarck.funFact: All threads come together: technology + analysis + security + coordination = the perfect role.
